lostDestinations.source: Post-Journal 2018: DOT terminated EAS subsidy Jan 2018 citing <10 pax/day

note: Airport lost EAS designation Jan 2018; Southern Airways Express was final EAS carrier flying to PIT; airport now general aviation only

lostDestinations.source: North Country Now 2024: Breeze scrapped IAD flights, switched to RDU hub
Massena Airport Wikipedia: Pittsburgh EAS service via Watertown ended 2007
Massena Airport Wikipedia: Delta Connection BOS service ended 2008, Cape Air then served Albany route

note: OGS now EAS-served by Breeze Airways to RDU; previously had USAir Express PIT and Delta Connection BOS service

lostDestinations.source: Massena International Airport Wikipedia: USAir Express PIT service via OGS and ART ended 2007
Massena Airport Wikipedia: Brockway Air/Piedmont Commuter route included Syracuse-Burlington stops
Massena Airport Wikipedia: Piedmont Commuter BOS-SYR route with stops at BTV, PBG, MSS, OGS, ART ended July 1991
Massena Airport Wikipedia: Cape Air served MSS-ALB from 2008; Boutique Air replaced with BOS service 2017

note: MSS now EAS-served by Boutique Air to BOS; historical USAir Express served PIT route; Piedmont Commuter linked to SYR/BOS corridor

lostDestinations.source: nny360.com: American served ART to Chicago until 2015 then switched to PHL
Massena Airport Wikipedia: USAir Express PIT service via OGS and ART ended 2007
Massena Airport Wikipedia: Delta Connection BOS-ART stop ended 2008

note: ART remains EAS-served by American Eagle to PHL; Chicago service replaced by PHL circa 2015 per DOT EAS contract

lostDestinations.source: The Points Guy: United ended ELM-ORD in 2016; previously launched 2014
United drops Elmira NY mytwintiers.com: United EWR service 2018, switched to IAD Mar 2019
The Points Guy: United ended ELM-IAD Jan 6 2020
Chemung County Historical Society blog: Mohawk/Allegheny/USAir flew to Elmira until 2001 when affiliate took over
Chemung County Historical Society blog: USAir/US Airways express previously served PIT hub

note: ELM currently served by Delta to DTW and Allegiant to leisure destinations; Charlotte CLT service existed but was cut ~2022 when AA exited region

lostDestinations.source: Plattsburgh International Airport Wikipedia: Colgan Air BOS service until June 2012; PenAir replaced; service shifted to PHL under EAS
Plattsburgh Airport / Lake Placid News Feb 2025: Contour switched from PHL to IAD EAS route May 2025

note: PBG now EAS-served by Contour to IAD (Dulles) since May 2025; previously to PHL 2022-2025; Breeze Airways also briefly served MCO/TPA 2023-2025

lostDestinations.source: wour.com Utica aviation history: Allegheny DC-9s served UCA to PIT hub until 1979; USAir continued briefly
Empire Airlines (1976-1985) Wikipedia: Empire certified UCA-BOS route; merged into Piedmont 1986
Oneida County Airport Wikipedia: commuter service to NYC metro served via EWR
Oneida County Airport Wikipedia: CommutAir LGA service ended June 30 2002; EAS subsidy exceeded $200/pax cap

note: UCA airport closed Jan 2007, operations moved to Griffiss Intl (RME) in Rome NY; last commercial service ended 2002 when CommutAir exited due to EAS cost cap

lostDestinations.source: Floyd Bennett Memorial Airport Wikipedia: Allegheny served GFL 1970s; Air North until early 1980s
Floyd Bennett Memorial Airport Wikipedia: Allegheny served GFL 1970s with connections to PHL hub
Floyd Bennett Memorial Airport Wikipedia/search: Air North served regional routes including Albany corridor through early 1980s

note: GFL lost all commercial service by mid-1980s when Air North folded; now general aviation only; Mohawk/Allegheny/Air North all served before deregulation ended viability

lostDestinations.source: Ithaca Voice June 2022: AA ended ITH-PHL effective Sept 6 2022, citing regional pilot shortage
14850.com: Delta discontinued ITH-DTW service, replaced with additional JFK flights
14850.com Feb 2024: Ithaca regained PHL dropping daily CLT service; CLT was added when PHL was cut ~2020

detail: Ithaca Tompkins lost American Airlines Philadelphia service (Sep 2022) and Delta Detroit service; Cornell University drives remaining demand; airport now served by Delta to JFK and United to IAD only.
source: Ithaca Voice 2022; 14850.com 2024; Tompkins County press release 2022

lostDestinations.source: thetravel.com/simpleflying.com: American exited BGM early 2017, operated PHL route on Dash 8 turboprops
thetravel.com: United Express ended BGM-EWR late 2016 on Saab 340 turboprops
ifly.com BGM: USAir operated DC-9/B737/Fokker jets BGM-PIT as hub connector through 1990s

detail: Binghamton was abandoned by United (2016), American (2017), leaving only Delta's Detroit route; Delta itself threatened to exit Feb 2025 but reversed course and restored service May 2026 after a $52M terminal renovation.

lostDestinations.source: Hudson Valley Regional Airport Wikipedia: last service ended Aug 12 2001, CommutAir discontinued Burlington flights
metroairportnews.com: Command Airways, headquartered at POU, became American Eagle; hub operations ended when Stewart Airport opened 1990
Hudson Valley Regional Airport Wikipedia: CommutAir served BUF-WPB-POU-BTV-PBG route through mid-1990s

detail: Poughkeepsie lost its last scheduled service Aug 12 2001 when CommutAir discontinued Burlington flights; service had already collapsed after Stewart International Airport opened across the Hudson in 1990 and captured most commercial traffic.
source: Hudson Valley Regional Airport Wikipedia; metroairportnews.com

lostDestinations.source: Adirondack Regional Airport website: Cape Air operates SLK-BOS daily EAS route
Cape Air Wikipedia: Cape Air began SLK EAS from Boston Feb 12 2008, replacing earlier Albany-focused routes

detail: Adirondack Regional Airport (SLK) is fully EAS-dependent; Cape Air retained as operator in Feb 2026 DOT contract decision over competing bids from SkyWest and Contour; serves BOS and JFK daily with Cessna 402 aircraft.
source: Lake Placid News Feb 2026: NY DOT sticks with Cape Air at Lake Clear airport; adirondackairport.com

lostDestinations.source: Niagara Falls International Airport Wikipedia: Vision Airlines d/b/a People Express ended all service Sep 26 2014
Niagara Falls International Airport Wikipedia: FLL service began Dec 2010, ended with People Express Sep 2014
Niagara Falls International Airport Wikipedia: Air Niagara flew IAG-EWR and IAG-JFK 1982-1984 with B727-100s
Niagara Falls International Airport Wikipedia: Spirit suspended IAG flights Oct 2020

detail: Niagara Falls International once hosted Air Niagara jet service (1982-84), Empire Airlines (mid-1980s), and Spirit Airlines (2011-2020); now only Allegiant serves seasonal leisure markets; proximity to Buffalo and Toronto airports severely constrains demand.
source: Niagara Falls International Airport Wikipedia; military-history.fandom.com
